# Risk Assessment — 2026-06-22

**Stack:** Node.js, TypeScript, React, VS Code Extension, OpenCode, MCP, npm
**Feeds:** Zero Day Initiative, CISA KEV, Anthropic Red Team, Google Threat Intelligence, Krebs on Security
**Generated by:** threat-monitor skill

---

## Landscape Summary

June 2026: AI-powered vulnerability discovery is accelerating at an unprecedented pace. Anthropic's Frontier Red Team published multiple studies showing LLMs can discover and exploit N-day/0-day vulnerabilities with growing capability (Project Fetch Phase 2, N-day exploit evaluation, Claude Mythos Preview). Microsoft's June Patch Tuesday set a record with nearly 200 fixes — the company explicitly attributed the surge to AI-assisted bug discovery. Two critical 0-day command injection vulnerabilities (CVSS 9.8) were disclosed in aws-mcp-server with no authentication required. An active npm supply chain campaign by DPRK-linked UNC1069 continues, and Google TI reported China-nexus UNC6508 targeting AI/medical/defense research. CISA's KEV catalog reached 1,623 actively exploited vulnerabilities. VS Code itself had a zero-day allowing GitHub token theft with a single click. The convergence of AI vulnerability discovery and active supply chain attacks makes dependency hygiene and MCP server security the top priorities for agentic AI tooling projects.

---

## IMMEDIATE (ACT NOW)

### [CVSS 9.8] ZDI-26-245/246: aws-mcp-server Command Injection RCE
- **Source:** Zero Day Initiative · Apr 2026
- **CVE:** CVE-2026-5058, CVE-2026-5059
- **Auth required?** No
- **Relevance:** DIRECT — opencode-chat connects to an OpenCode server which can run MCP servers including aws-mcp-server. Exploitation allows unauthenticated remote code execution via AWS CLI command injection.
- **Action:** Verify no aws-mcp-server is running in the user's OpenCode MCP configuration. Review all MCP server definitions for shell command injection patterns. Run `opencode mcp list` to audit active MCP servers.

### VS Code GitHub Token Theft Zero-Day
- **Source:** Krebs on Security · Jun 2026
- **CVE:** CVE-2026-49160 (Patch Tuesday)
- **Relevance:** DIRECT — opencode-chat is a VS Code extension. A zero-day in VS Code allowed attackers to steal GitHub tokens with a single click. Microsoft pushed a stopgap fix on June 3 after public exploit was published.
- **Action:** Ensure VS Code is updated to latest version. Rotate any GitHub tokens used in VS Code settings/extensions. Verify extension is not requesting unnecessary OAuth scopes.

### npm Supply Chain Attack — Axios/UNC1069
- **Source:** Google Threat Intelligence · Mar 2026
- **Relevance:** MEDIUM — Active DPRK-linked campaign compromises npm packages including Axios (widely used in JavaScript ecosystem). Uses PowerShell droppers and credential stealers.
- **Action:** Audit all npm dependencies. pnpm audit currently passes but supply chain attacks evolve rapidly. Consider using `pnpm audit --audit-level=moderate` regularly.

---

## HIGH (THIS WEEK)

### AI Vulnerability Discovery Accelerating
- **Source:** Anthropic Red Team · Jun 2026
- **Finding:** Project Fetch Phase 2 (Jun 18) shows continued progress in LLM-based vulnerability discovery. "Measuring LLMs' impact on N-day exploits" (Jun 8) and "Measuring LLMs' ability to develop exploits" (May 22) confirm AI models are finding and weaponizing vulnerabilities faster than traditional researchers. Microsoft confirms AI-assisted discovery drove record Patch Tuesday volume.
- **Relevance:** HIGH — The code in this repo is JavaScript/TypeScript which is within the scope of AI vulnerability discovery. Auto-generated patches from Dependabot may become more frequent.
- **Action:** Keep Dependabot enabled. Run security audit after every dependency update batch. Monitor Anthropic Red Team publications for methodology shifts.

### China-Nexus UNC6508 Targeting AI/Medical/Defense
- **Source:** Google Threat Intelligence · Jun 2026
- **Finding:** PRC-nexus threat actor compromised externally facing web applications, deployed bespoke malware, and exfiltrated data from AI, cyber, medical, and defense research organizations. Undetected for over a year.
- **Relevance:** MEDIUM — opencode-chat is an open-source VS Code extension. Supply chain compromise vectors (malicious PRs, compromised dependencies) are relevant.
- **Action:** Review PRs carefully especially from new contributors. Verify dependency integrity via lockfile. Dependabot helps but doesn't catch all supply chain attacks.

### LiteLLM / PyPI Credential Stealer Campaign
- **Source:** Google Threat Intelligence · Recent
- **Finding:** TeamPCP compromised PyPI packages and GitHub repositories to embed SANDCLOCK credential stealer, extracting AWS keys and GitHub tokens from build environments. LiteLLM (AI gateway for LLM providers) was among the compromised packages.
- **Relevance:** MEDIUM — The AI/agent ecosystem is an active target. While this campaign targets PyPI, similar patterns apply to npm.
- **Action:** Monitor npm for compromised packages. Review GitHub Actions secrets exposure. Enable branch protection rules if not already active.

---

## WATCH (TREND)

### Record Patch Tuesday Volume (200+ fixes)
- **Source:** Krebs on Security · Jun 2026 · Microsoft attributes surge to AI-assisted bug discovery. 3 zero-days, 35+ critical-rated fixes. Pandora's box has opened — high patch volumes expected to be the norm.

### Popa Botnet / Residential Proxy Operations
- **Source:** Krebs on Security · Jun 2026 · 4-year Android botnet linked to publicly-traded Israeli firm. Millions of compromised TV boxes used for ad fraud, account takeover, data scraping. Highlights the maturity of cybercrime-as-a-service.

### CISA Contractor AWS GovCloud Key Leak
- **Source:** Krebs on Security · May 2026 · CISA contractor maintained public GitHub repo exposing AWS GovCloud credentials and internal CISA system deployment files. Demonstrates that even security agencies fail at credential hygiene.
- **Relevance:** Reminder to never commit credentials. Gitleaks is active in CI for this repo.
